97: // ─── Daemon ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
98: 
99: export class Daemon {
100: 	readonly port: number;
101: 	readonly configDir: string;
102: 	readonly socketPath: string;
103: 	readonly logPath: string;
104: 	readonly pidPath: string;
105: 
106: 	private httpServer: HttpServer | null = null;
107: 	private ipcServer: NetServer | null = null;
108: 	private ipcClients: Set<Socket> = new Set();
109: 	private projects: Map<string, ProjectInfo> = new Map();
110: 	private startTime: number = Date.now();
111: 	private clientCount = 0;
112: 	private shuttingDown = false;
113: 
114: 	// Enhanced daemon fields (Ticket 8.7)
115: 	private pinHash: string | null;
116: 	private readonly auth: AuthManager;
117: 	private tlsEnabled: boolean;
118: 	private keepAwake: boolean;
119: 
120: 	// Relay integration
121: 	private readonly opencodeUrl: string | null;
122: 	private readonly staticDir: string;
123: 	private projectRelays: Map<string, ProjectRelay> = new Map();
124: 	private pendingRelaySlugs = new Set<string>();
125: 	private pushManager: PushNotificationManager | null = null;
126: 
127: 	// Version checker (Ticket 3.4)
128: 	private versionChecker: VersionChecker | null = null;
129: 
130: 	// Keep-awake manager (Ticket 3.5)
131: 	private keepAwakeManager: KeepAwake | null = null;
132: 
133: 	// HTTP request router
134: 	private router: RequestRouter | null = null;
135: 
136: 	// Crash counter state
137: 	private crashTimestamps: number[] = [];
138: 
139: 	// Signal handler references (for cleanup)
140: 	private signalHandlers: { signal: string; handler: () => void }[] = [];

142: 	constructor(options?: DaemonOptions) {
143: 		const configDir = options?.configDir ?? DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR;
144: 		this.port = options?.port ?? DEFAULT_PORT;
145: 		this.configDir = configDir;
146: 		this.socketPath = options?.socketPath ?? join(configDir, "relay.sock");
147: 		this.logPath = options?.logPath ?? join(configDir, "daemon.log");
148: 		this.pidPath = options?.pidPath ?? join(configDir, "daemon.pid");
149: 		this.pinHash = options?.pinHash ?? null;
150: 		this.auth = new AuthManager();
151: 		if (this.pinHash) {
152: 			this.auth.setPinHash(this.pinHash);
153: 		}
154: 		this.tlsEnabled = options?.tlsEnabled ?? false;
155: 		this.keepAwake = options?.keepAwake ?? false;
156: 		this.opencodeUrl = options?.opencodeUrl ?? null;
157: 		this.staticDir =
158: 			options?.staticDir ?? join(process.cwd(), "dist", "public");
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	// ─── Lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
162: 
163: 	/** Start the daemon: HTTP server + IPC socket + signal handlers */
164: 	async start(): Promise<void> {
165: 		// Ensure config directory exists
166: 		if (!existsSync(this.configDir)) {
167: 			mkdirSync(this.configDir, { recursive: true });
168: 		}
169: 
170: 		// Record crash for crash-counter tracking
171: 		this.recordCrashTimestamp();
172: 
173: 		// Check crash counter
174: 		if (this.shouldGiveUp()) {
175: 			throw new Error(
176: 				`Daemon crashed ${MAX_CRASHES} times within ${CRASH_WINDOW_MS / 1000}s — giving up`,
177: 			);
178: 		}
179: 
180: 		// Write PID file
181: 		this.writePidFile();
182: 
183: 		// Initialize push notification manager (non-fatal if it fails)
184: 		try {
185: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
186: 			this.pushManager = new PushNotificationManager({
187: 				configDir: this.configDir,
188: 			});
189: 			await this.pushManager.init();
190: 		} catch (err) {
191: 			console.warn(
192: 				"[daemon] Push notifications unavailable:",
193: 				(err as Error).message,
194: 			);
195: 			this.pushManager = null;
196: 		}
197: 
198: 		// Create HTTP request router
199: 		this.router = new RequestRouter({
200: 			auth: this.auth,
201: 			staticDir: this.staticDir,
202: 			getProjects: () =>
203: 				this.getProjects().map((p) => ({
204: 					slug: p.slug,
205: 					directory: p.directory,
206: 					title: p.title,
207: 				})),
208: 			port: this.port,
209: 			isTls: this.tlsEnabled,
210: 			pushManager: this.pushManager ?? undefined,
211: 			authExemptPaths: ["/setup", "/health", "/api/status"],
212: 			getHealthResponse: () => this.getStatus(),
213: 		});
214: 
215: 		// Start HTTP server
216: 		await this.startHttpServer();
217: 
218: 		// WebSocket upgrade router — routes /p/{slug}/ws to the correct relay's WSS.
219: 		// Must be registered after HTTP server starts but before any relays are added.
220: 		this.httpServer!.on("upgrade", (req, socket, head) => {
221: 			const match = req.url?.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)\/ws(?:\?|$)/);
222: 			if (!match) {
223: 				socket.destroy();
224: 				return;
225: 			}
226: 			const slug = match[1]!;
227: 			const relay = this.projectRelays.get(slug);
228: 			if (!relay) {
229: 				socket.destroy();
230: 				return;
231: 			}
232: 			if (this.auth.hasPin() && !this.router!.checkAuth(req)) {
233: 				socket.destroy();
234: 				return;
235: 			}
236: 			relay.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
237: 		});
238: 
239: 		// Start IPC server
240: 		await this.startIPCServer();
241: 
242: 		// Install signal handlers
243: 		this.installSignalHandlers();
244: 
245: 		// Mark start time (resets crash window on success)
246: 		this.startTime = Date.now();
247: 
248: 		// Clear any previous crash info and save config (Ticket 8.7)
249: 		clearCrashInfo(this.configDir);
250: 		saveDaemonConfig(this.buildConfig(), this.configDir);
251: 
252: 		// Start version checker (non-fatal if it fails)
253: 		this.versionChecker = new VersionChecker({
254: 			enabled: !process.argv.includes("--no-update"),
255: 		});
256: 		this.versionChecker.on("update_available", ({ latest }) => {
257: 			// Broadcast to all connected browsers
258: 			for (const relay of this.projectRelays.values()) {
259: 				relay.wsHandler.broadcast({
260: 					type: "update_available",
261: 					version: latest,
262: 				});
263: 			}
264: 		});
265: 		this.versionChecker.start();
266: 
267: 		// Initialize keep-awake (macOS caffeinate)
268: 		this.keepAwakeManager = new KeepAwake({
269: 			enabled: this.keepAwake,
270: 		});
271: 	}

273: 	/** Gracefully stop the daemon */
274: 	async stop(): Promise<void> {
275: 		if (this.shuttingDown) return;
276: 		this.shuttingDown = true;
277: 
278: 		// Remove signal handlers
279: 		this.removeSignalHandlers();
280: 
281: 		// Stop version checker
282: 		this.versionChecker?.stop();
283: 		this.versionChecker = null;
284: 
285: 		// Stop keep-awake
286: 		this.keepAwakeManager?.deactivate();
287: 		this.keepAwakeManager = null;
288: 
289: 		// Clear daemon config before removing PID/socket files (Ticket 8.7)
290: 		clearDaemonConfig(this.configDir);
291: 
292: 		// Stop all relay pipelines
293: 		for (const relay of this.projectRelays.values()) {
294: 			try {
295: 				await relay.stop();
296: 			} catch {
297: 				// ignore
298: 			}
299: 		}
300: 		this.projectRelays.clear();
301: 
302: 		// Stop all projects
303: 		this.projects.clear();
304: 
305: 		// Close IPC clients
306: 		for (const client of this.ipcClients) {
307: 			try {
308: 				client.destroy();
309: 			} catch {
310: 				// ignore
311: 			}
312: 		}
313: 		this.ipcClients.clear();
314: 
315: 		// Close IPC server
316: 		await this.closeIPC();
317: 
318: 		// Close HTTP server
319: 		await this.closeHttp();
320: 
321: 		// Remove PID file
322: 		this.removePidFile();
323: 
324: 		// Remove socket file
325: 		this.removeSocketFile();
326: 
327: 		this.shuttingDown = false;
328: 	}

330: 	// ─── Project management ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
331: 
332: 	/** Add a project, generating a slug if needed */
333: 	async addProject(directory: string, slug?: string): Promise<ProjectInfo> {
334: 		// Normalize to absolute path with no trailing slash — OpenCode's Instance
335: 		// cache uses the raw directory string as key with NO normalization.
336: 		directory = resolve(directory);
337: 
338: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(this.projects.keys());
339: 
340: 		// Check if directory is already registered
341: 		for (const project of this.projects.values()) {
342: 			if (project.directory === directory) {
343: 				return project;
344: 			}
345: 		}
346: 
347: 		const resolvedSlug = slug ?? generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
348: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
349: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
350: 
351: 		const project: ProjectInfo = {
352: 			slug: resolvedSlug,
353: 			directory,
354: 			title,
355: 			lastUsed: Date.now(),
356: 		};
357: 
358: 		this.projects.set(resolvedSlug, project);
359: 
360: 		// Persist config and sync recent projects (Ticket 8.7)
361: 		saveDaemonConfig(this.buildConfig(), this.configDir);
362: 		syncRecentProjects(
363: 			this.getProjects().map((p) => ({
364: 				path: p.directory,
365: 				slug: p.slug,
366: 				title: p.title,
367: 			})),
368: 			this.configDir,
369: 		);
370: 
371: 		// Start relay pipeline for this project if OpenCode is available.
372: 		// Each project gets its own relay with noServer mode — the daemon's
373: 		// upgrade handler routes WebSocket connections by slug.
374: 		if (
375: 			this.opencodeUrl &&
376: 			this.httpServer &&
377: 			!this.projectRelays.has(resolvedSlug) &&
378: 			!this.pendingRelaySlugs.has(resolvedSlug)
379: 		) {
380: 			this.pendingRelaySlugs.add(resolvedSlug);
381: 			try {
382: 				const { createProjectRelay } = await import("./relay-stack.js");
383: 				const relay = await createProjectRelay({
384: 					httpServer: this.httpServer,
385: 					opencodeUrl: this.opencodeUrl,
386: 					projectDir: directory,
387: 					slug: resolvedSlug,
388: 					noServer: true,
389: 					sessionTitle: "Relay Session",
390: 					log: (...args: unknown[]) => console.log("[relay]", ...args),
391: 					// No verifyClient — daemon's upgrade handler checks auth
392: 					getProjects: () => this.getProjects(),
393: 					addProject: async (dir: string) => {
394: 						const p = await this.addProject(dir);
395: 						return {
396: 							slug: p.slug,
397: 							title: p.title,
398: 							directory: p.directory,
399: 						};
400: 					},
401: 					pushManager: this.pushManager ?? undefined,
402: 				});
403: 				this.projectRelays.set(resolvedSlug, relay);
404: 			} catch (err) {
405: 				// Non-fatal: relay won't work but daemon continues serving pages
406: 				console.error(
407: 					"[daemon] Failed to start relay:",
408: 					(err as Error).message,
409: 				);
410: 			} finally {
411: 				this.pendingRelaySlugs.delete(resolvedSlug);
412: 			}
413: 		}
414: 
415: 		return project;
416: 	}

418: 	/** Remove a project by slug */
419: 	async removeProject(slug: string): Promise<void> {
420: 		if (!this.projects.has(slug)) {
421: 			throw new Error(`Project "${slug}" not found`);
422: 		}
423: 		this.projects.delete(slug);
424: 
425: 		// Stop and remove relay for this project (prevents orphaned SSE/WS resources)
426: 		const relay = this.projectRelays.get(slug);
427: 		if (relay) {
428: 			try {
429: 				await relay.stop();
430: 			} catch {
431: 				// ignore
432: 			}
433: 			this.projectRelays.delete(slug);
434: 		}
435: 
436: 		// Persist config and sync recent projects (Ticket 8.7)
437: 		saveDaemonConfig(this.buildConfig(), this.configDir);
438: 		syncRecentProjects(
439: 			this.getProjects().map((p) => ({
440: 				path: p.directory,
441: 				slug: p.slug,
442: 				title: p.title,
443: 			})),
444: 			this.configDir,
445: 		);
446: 	}
447: 
448: 	/** Get all registered projects */
449: 	getProjects(): ProjectInfo[] {
450: 		return Array.from(this.projects.values());
451: 	}

453: 	// ─── Status ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
454: 
455: 	/** Get daemon status */
456: 	getStatus(): DaemonStatus {
457: 		return {
458: 			ok: true,
459: 			uptime: (Date.now() - this.startTime) / 1000,
460: 			port: this.port,
461: 			projectCount: this.projects.size,
462: 			clientCount: this.clientCount,
463: 			pinEnabled: this.pinHash !== null,
464: 			tlsEnabled: this.tlsEnabled,
465: 			keepAwake: this.keepAwake,
466: 			projects: this.getProjects().map((p) => ({
467: 				slug: p.slug,
468: 				directory: p.directory,
469: 				title: p.title,
470: 				lastUsed: p.lastUsed,
471: 			})),
472: 		};
473: 	}

475: 	// ─── Private: Config building (Ticket 8.7) ─────────────────────────────
476: 
477: 	private buildConfig(): DaemonConfig {
478: 		return {
479: 			pid: process.pid,
480: 			port: this.port,
481: 			pinHash: this.pinHash,
482: 			tls: this.tlsEnabled,
483: 			debug: false,
484: 			keepAwake: this.keepAwake,
485: 			dangerouslySkipPermissions: false,
486: 			projects: this.getProjects().map((p) => ({
487: 				path: p.directory,
488: 				slug: p.slug,
489: 				title: p.title,
490: 				addedAt: p.lastUsed ?? Date.now(),
491: 			})),
492: 		};
493: 	}

495: 	// ─── Static methods ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
496: 
497: 	/** Check if a daemon is already running */
498: 	static async isRunning(socketPath?: string): Promise<boolean> {
499: 		const resolvedSocketPath =
500: 			socketPath ?? join(DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR, "relay.sock");
501: 		const pidPath = resolvedSocketPath.replace(/relay\.sock$/, "daemon.pid");
502: 
503: 		// Check PID file
504: 		let pid: number | null = null;
505: 		try {
506: 			const content = readFileSync(pidPath, "utf-8").trim();
507: 			pid = Number.parseInt(content, 10);
508: 		} catch {
509: 			// No PID file — not running
510: 			return false;
511: 		}
512: 
513: 		if (pid === null || Number.isNaN(pid)) {
514: 			// Invalid PID file — stale
515: 			Daemon.cleanupStale(pidPath, resolvedSocketPath);
516: 			return false;
517: 		}
518: 
519: 		// Check if PID is alive
520: 		try {
521: 			process.kill(pid, 0);
522: 		} catch {
523: 			// Process doesn't exist — stale
524: 			Daemon.cleanupStale(pidPath, resolvedSocketPath);
525: 			return false;
526: 		}
527: 
528: 		// PID is alive, but verify via socket connection
529: 		const { connect } = await import("node:net");
530: 		return new Promise((resolve) => {
531: 			const client = connect(resolvedSocketPath);
532: 			const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
533: 				client.destroy();
534: 				resolve(false);
535: 			}, 2000);
536: 
537: 			client.on("connect", () => {
538: 				clearTimeout(timeout);
539: 				client.destroy();
540: 				resolve(true);
541: 			});
542: 
543: 			client.on("error", () => {
544: 				clearTimeout(timeout);
545: 				Daemon.cleanupStale(pidPath, resolvedSocketPath);
546: 				resolve(false);
547: 			});
548: 		});
549: 	}

551: 	/**
552: 	 * Build spawn configuration without actually spawning.
553: 	 * Exposed as a static method so tests can verify the config shape.
554: 	 * Delegates to daemon-spawn.ts.
555: 	 */
556: 	static buildSpawnConfig(options?: DaemonOptions): SpawnConfig {
557: 		return buildSpawnConfigImpl(options);
558: 	}

560: 	/** Spawn a new daemon as a detached background process. Delegates to daemon-spawn.ts. */
561: 	static async spawn(
562: 		options?: DaemonOptions,
563: 	): Promise<{ pid: number; port: number }> {
564: 		return spawnDaemon(options, Daemon.isRunning);
565: 	}

567: 	// ─── Private: HTTP Server ───────────────────────────────────────────────
568: 
569: 	private startHttpServer(): Promise<void> {
570: 		return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
571: 			this.httpServer = createServer((req, res) => {
572: 				this.router!.handleRequest(req, res).catch((err) => {
573: 					console.error("[daemon] Request error:", err);
574: 					if (!res.headersSent) {
575: 						res.writeHead(500, { "Content-Type": "text/plain" });
576: 						res.end("Internal Server Error");
577: 					}
578: 				});
579: 			});
580: 
581: 			this.httpServer.on("error", (err) => {
582: 				reject(err);
583: 			});
584: 
585: 			this.httpServer.listen(this.port, "0.0.0.0", () => {
586: 				resolve();
587: 			});
588: 		});
589: 	}
590: 
591: 	private closeHttp(): Promise<void> {
592: 		return new Promise((resolve) => {
593: 			if (!this.httpServer) {
594: 				resolve();
595: 				return;
596: 			}
597: 
598: 			const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
599: 				resolve();
600: 			}, SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS);
601: 
602: 			this.httpServer.close(() => {
603: 				clearTimeout(timeout);
604: 				this.httpServer = null;
605: 				resolve();
606: 			});
607: 		});
608: 	}

610: 	// ─── Private: IPC Server ────────────────────────────────────────────────
611: 
612: 	private startIPCServer(): Promise<void> {
613: 		return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
614: 			// Remove stale socket file if it exists
615: 			this.removeSocketFile();
616: 
617: 			const handlers = buildIPCHandlers(
618: 				{
619: 					addProject: (dir) => this.addProject(dir),
620: 					removeProject: (slug) => this.removeProject(slug),
621: 					getProjects: () => this.getProjects(),
622: 					getProjectBySlug: (slug) => this.projects.get(slug),
623: 					setProjectTitle: (slug, title) => {
624: 						const project = this.projects.get(slug);
625: 						if (project) project.title = title;
626: 					},
627: 					getPinHash: () => this.pinHash,
628: 					setPinHash: (hash) => {
629: 						this.pinHash = hash;
630: 						this.auth.setPinHash(hash);
631: 					},
632: 					getKeepAwake: () => this.keepAwake,

<content>1: // ─── Storage Monitor (Ticket 6.2 AC8) ───────────────────────────────────────
2: // Periodically checks available disk space and emits events on transitions
3: // between low/ok states. Used by the Daemon to warn about disk space issues.
4: 
5: import { EventEmitter } from "node:events";
6: import { statfs as nodeStatfs } from "node:fs/promises";
7: 
8: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: export interface StorageMonitorOptions {
11: 	/** Path to check disk space for */
12: 	path: string;
13: 	/** Threshold in bytes below which low_disk_space is emitted (default: 100MB) */
14: 	thresholdBytes?: number;
15: 	/** Polling interval in milliseconds (default: 5 minutes) */
16: 	intervalMs?: number;
17: 	/** Injectable statfs for testing — defaults to wrapping Node.js fs.statfs() */
18: 	_statfs?: (path: string) => Promise<{ available: number }>;
19: }
20: 
21: export interface LowDiskSpaceEvent {
22: 	availableBytes: number;
23: 	thresholdBytes: number;
24: }
25: 
26: export interface DiskSpaceOkEvent {
27: 	availableBytes: number;
28: }
29: 
30: // ─── Defaults ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31: 
32: const DEFAULT_THRESHOLD_BYTES = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
33: const DEFAULT_INTERVAL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes
34: 
35: // ─── Default statfs wrapper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: async function defaultStatfs(path: string): Promise<{ available: number }> {
38: 	const stats = await nodeStatfs(path);
39: 	return { available: stats.bavail * stats.bsize };
40: }
41: 
42: // ─── StorageMonitor ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43: 
44: export class StorageMonitor extends EventEmitter {
45: 	private readonly monitorPath: string;
46: 	private readonly thresholdBytes: number;
47: 	private readonly intervalMs: number;
48: 	private readonly statfsFn: (path: string) => Promise<{ available: number }>;
49: 
50: 	private timer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
51: 	private wasLow: boolean | null = null; // null = no check yet
52: 	private checking = false;
53: 
54: 	constructor(options: StorageMonitorOptions) {
55: 		super();
56: 		this.monitorPath = options.path;
57: 		this.thresholdBytes = options.thresholdBytes ?? DEFAULT_THRESHOLD_BYTES;
58: 		this.intervalMs = options.intervalMs ?? DEFAULT_INTERVAL_MS;
59: 		this.statfsFn = options._statfs ?? defaultStatfs;
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
63: 
64: 	/** Start periodic polling. First check runs immediately, then on interval. */
65: 	start(): void {
66: 		// Run the first check immediately
67: 		void this.check();
68: 
69: 		// Set up periodic polling
70: 		this.timer = setInterval(() => {
71: 			void this.check();
72: 		}, this.intervalMs);
73: 
74: 		// Don't keep the process alive just for this timer
75: 		this.timer.unref();
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	/** Stop polling (idempotent). */
79: 	stop(): void {
80: 		if (this.timer !== null) {
81: 			clearInterval(this.timer);
82: 			this.timer = null;
83: 		}
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	// ─── Private ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
87: 
88: 	private async check(): Promise<void> {
89: 		if (this.checking) return;
90: 		this.checking = true;
91: 		let available: number;
92: 		try {
93: 			({ available } = await this.statfsFn(this.monitorPath));
94: 		} finally {
95: 			this.checking = false;
96: 		}
97: 		const isLow = available < this.thresholdBytes;
98: 
99: 		if (isLow && this.wasLow !== true) {
100: 			// Transition to low (from ok/unknown)
101: 			this.wasLow = true;
102: 			this.emit("low_disk_space", {
103: 				availableBytes: available,
104: 				thresholdBytes: this.thresholdBytes,
105: 			} satisfies LowDiskSpaceEvent);
106: 		} else if (!isLow && this.wasLow === true) {
107: 			// Transition to ok (from low)
108: 			this.wasLow = false;
109: 			this.emit("disk_space_ok", {
110: 				availableBytes: available,
111: 			} satisfies DiskSpaceOkEvent);
112: 		} else if (!isLow && this.wasLow === null) {
113: 			// First check is ok — just record state, no event
114: 			this.wasLow = false;
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: }
